quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, was named after a Greek sailor who sailed under a Spanish name! His real name was Ioannis Phokas, but he's remembered by his Spanish alias. He claimed to have sailed through this waterway in 1592, though historians still debate whether he actually did.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals, common visitors to the Strait of Juan de Fuca, have whiskers so sensitive they can track fish in complete darkness. Learn about these charming marine neighbors and their remarkable abilities.

medium_read.title: The Unexpected Benefits of Talking to Your Plants
medium_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that plants respond to vibrations, including human voices, in fascinating ways. This gentle exploration reveals what really happens when gardeners chat with their green friends.

long_read.title: The Hidden World of Old-Growth Forests in Olympic National Park
long_read.teaser: Deep in the forests near Port Angeles lies an ancient ecosystem where trees communicate through underground networks and nurse logs give life to new generations. Discover the magic happening in your own backyard.

port_angeles.title: Butterfly Season at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot butterflies at Hurricane Ridge! Species like the Anise Swallowtail and Mourning Cloak flutter among the wildflowers that are blooming right now. The meadows are coming alive with color and movement.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! You got it! Those tiny hearts are incredibly powerful, beating about 1,200 times per minute — that's 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute during flight — such a remarkable little creature!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These amazing little birds have hearts that beat about 1,200 times per minute in flight — imagine the energy that takes!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of any warm-blooded animal. To fuel their rapid heartbeat and constant motion, they must eat about half their body weight in sugar daily!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly in Hungarian — such a lovely, delicate-sounding word for such a delicate creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound as light and airy as a butterfly itself? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian language is full of beautiful nature words. Pillangó comes from an old word meaning 'to flutter' — perfectly capturing how butterflies dance through the air!

puzzle_3.question: What tree species can live for over 1,000 years in the Olympic Peninsula rainforests?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Western Redcedar can live well over 1,000 years — some of the trees in Olympic National Park were already ancient when Columbus sailed to America!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the Western Redcedar that can live over 1,000 years! These magnificent trees were sacred to Indigenous peoples and provided materials for canoes, clothing, and longhouses.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Western Redcedar was called the 'Tree of Life' by Coast Salish peoples. Its rot-resistant wood and the tree's incredible longevity made it central to Pacific Northwest cultures for thousands of years.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
